author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
hari hari! kabe haba vṛndāvanavāsī
nirakhiba nayane jugala⋅rūparāśi

tyajiyā śayanasukha vicitra pālaṅka
kabe vrajera dhūlāya dhūsara ha’be aṅga

ṣaḍrasa bhojana dūre parihari
kabe vraje māgiyā khāiba mādhukarī

parikramā kariyā beḍāba vane vane
viśrāma kariyā jāi jamunā⋅puline

tāpa dūra kariba śītala vaṁśīvaṭe
(kabe) kuñje baiṭhaba hāma vaiṣṇava⋅nikaṭe

narottama⋅dāsa kahe kari’ parihāra
kabe vā emana daśā hoibe āmāra
en_translation:
Hari Hari! When will I become a resident of Vṛndāvana? I will behold with my eyes the beautiful forms of the Divine Couple.

Forsaking the comfort of sleeping on an elaborate bed, when will my body be covered in the dust of Vraja?

Leaving aside meals with six tastes (sweet, sour, salty, pungent, bitter, and astringent), when will I eat by begging for mādhukarī (alms) in Vraja?

I will wander from forest to forest performing parikramā, and I will rest on the banks of the Yamunā.

I will relieve my distress in the cool Vaṁśīvaṭa, when will I sit in a kuñja (grove) in the company of Vaiṣṇavas?

Narottam Dāsa says, making this humble plea, when will such a state be mine?
